name: ALPR engine sidecar (Docker) + Makefile targets + Go client
summary: Containerized ALPR engine running as a separate Docker service under a 'profiles: [alpr]' guard so it never starts unless the user explicitly opts in. The Go backend is a pure HTTP client of the engine -- image bytes in, plate detections out. This feature also ships the Makefile targets that make enabling ALPR a single command (make alpr-up) and matches the existing 'as simple as the postgres container' deployment pattern. The engine choice (FastALPR / OpenALPR-OSS / DIY) was decided in alpr-engine-spike; follow that decision document.
acceptance_criteria: New directory docker/alpr/ containing: Dockerfile for the chosen engine (CPU-friendly default; document GPU variant in docs/ALPR.md), an entrypoint script if the engine doesn't ship one, a small wrapper service exposing the HTTP API below if the chosen engine doesn't expose one natively. The Dockerfile pins all model files and Python deps to specific versions so the build is reproducible.

HTTP API inside the container (port 8081): GET /health -> 200 {ok: true, model: '<name>', version: '<v>', region: '<us|eu|...>', supports_attributes: bool}. POST /v1/detect (multipart form-data with image OR application/json {image_b64}) -> 200 {detections: [{plate: string, confidence: float (0..1), bbox: {x,y,w,h}, ...}]}. 4xx on invalid input. 5s server-side timeout per request.

docker-compose.yml gains a service named alpr under profiles: [alpr] (so it does NOT start with bare `docker compose up`). Configure: image (built locally for now), expose 8081 only on the internal compose network (no host port required for prod -- include a commented-out ports stanza for dev), healthcheck hitting /health every 30s, restart: unless-stopped, depends_on: nothing (alpr is independent). GPU device_requests stanza commented out with a pointer to docs/ALPR.md for activation.

Makefile gains 5 targets, each documented with ## comments matching the existing style: alpr-up (`docker compose --profile alpr up -d alpr`), alpr-down (`docker compose --profile alpr stop alpr && docker compose --profile alpr rm -f alpr`), alpr-build (`docker compose --profile alpr build alpr`), alpr-logs (`docker compose --profile alpr logs -f alpr`), alpr-pull (`docker compose --profile alpr pull alpr`). Update the .PHONY line at the top of the Makefile to include all new targets. Verify `make help` shows the new targets correctly.
Targets compose with the prod stack: `make prod-up && make alpr-up` brings up the full stack including the sidecar; alpr-up does NOT depend on prod-up so dev usage works too.

New Go package internal/alpr with a Client: NewClient(baseURL string, timeout time.Duration) *Client; Client.Detect(ctx, frameJPEG []byte) ([]Detection, error); Client.Health(ctx) (HealthInfo, error). Returns typed errors so callers can degrade gracefully: ErrEngineUnreachable (DNS/connection refused), ErrEngineTimeout (context deadline), ErrEngineBadResponse (non-2xx or unparseable). Do NOT panic on engine errors -- log at warn (rate-limited if there's no rate-limiter available, OR add a basic one) and bubble up.

Detection struct: PlateText string, Confidence float64, BBox Rect, Vehicle *VehicleAttributes (filled by alpr-vehicle-attributes-engine; nullable here). VehicleAttributes struct lives in this package as a forward-compatible placeholder.

cmd/server only constructs internal/alpr.Client when the runtime alpr_enabled flag is true OR keeps a lazily-initialized client that workers/handlers acquire on demand (preferred -- avoids restarting on toggle). Wire via the deps struct in cmd/server/deps.go.

Pointer paragraph added to README.md 'Setup' section (or new 'Optional services' subsection): one paragraph noting ALPR is an opt-in feature, link to docs/ALPR.md (full doc owned by alpr-docs feature). Do NOT duplicate the long disclaimer text here.

Smoke verification: with the alpr profile NOT active, `docker compose ps` shows alpr absent and `make prod-up` works exactly as today. With `make alpr-up`, `curl http://localhost:8081/health` returns ok (port published for dev only). `make alpr-down` stops cleanly and removes the container.
Tests for internal/alpr.Client: mock HTTP server returns canned responses for /health and /v1/detect; verify success path, timeout, unreachable, malformed JSON each map to the right typed error.
Image tag conventions: tag locally-built images as `comma-alpr:dev` and `comma-alpr:<git-short-sha>`; document in docs/ALPR.md so users can pin versions.
notes: Implemented on agent/alpr-engine-service against feat/alpr. docker/alpr/ ships a uv-managed FastAPI wrapper around fast-alpr (pinned: fast-alpr 0.1.5, onnxruntime 1.20.1, model identifiers exposed as ARG/ENV per docs/ALPR.md). docker-compose.yml gains the `alpr` profile (no host port published in prod, commented-out 8081 mapping for dev, healthcheck against /health every 30s, GPU device_requests stanza commented with a pointer to docs/ALPR.md). Makefile gains alpr-up/down/build/logs/pull, all listed by `make help`. internal/alpr.Client exposes Detect / Health with typed sentinel errors (ErrEngineUnreachable, ErrEngineTimeout, ErrEngineBadResponse); rate-limited warn log on unreachable. cmd/server/deps.go gains a lazy ALPRClient() accessor so toggling alpr_enabled at runtime does not require a restart. 12 unit tests pass (success / timeout / context-deadline / unreachable / 4xx / 5xx / malformed-json / empty-frame / construction defaults). docs/ALPR.md is a stub per the brief; the full operator doc is owned by alpr-docs. Docker engine end-to-end (`make alpr-build`) was NOT exercised in this worktree -- the wrapper compose config + Dockerfile are syntax-validated via `docker compose --profile alpr config` only.
